description: HEXACO Short-Form Item Bank — 96 items, 4 per facet, 24 facets across 6 domains. Novel, non-copyrighted items. 5-point Likert scale (1=Strongly Disagree, 5=Strongly Agree). Reverse-scored items: response = 6 - raw.

items.text: I say what I genuinely think, even when my opinion isn't what others want to hear.
I adjust my expressed opinions depending on who I'm talking to.
I present myself to others exactly as I am, without embellishment.
I sometimes tell people what they want to hear rather than what I actually believe.
I would not take credit for something I didn't do, even if no one would find out.
I occasionally bend the rules when it benefits me and the risk of getting caught is low.
I hold myself to the same standards I expect of others.
If a cashier gave me too much change, I would likely keep it.
I don't place much value on owning expensive or luxurious things.
I would be willing to compromise my ethics if the financial reward were large enough.
Being wealthy is not something I think about much.
I sometimes feel envious of people who have more material things than I do.
I don't think of myself as particularly special compared to other people.
I deserve more recognition than people typically give me.
I am uncomfortable when others lavish me with excessive praise.
People who don't know me well would probably be impressed by my credentials and accomplishments.
The possibility of physical harm is enough to deter me from risky activities.
I rarely worry about getting hurt in dangerous situations.
I am more cautious about personal safety than most people I know.
Extreme sports or high-risk activities appeal to me.
I worry a great deal about things that might go wrong in the future.
I tend not to stress about problems until they actually occur.
I feel nervous or uneasy even when things seem to be going fine.
I rarely find myself lying awake at night because of worry.
When I'm under a lot of pressure, I need emotional support from others.
I cope with stress just as well when I'm alone as when others are around.
I find it hard to deal with difficult situations without someone to lean on.
I rarely need reassurance when facing personal challenges.
I feel strong emotional connections to the people I care about.
I tend not to get emotionally invested in other people's problems.
The welfare of my close friends and family affects me deeply.
I don't typically feel moved by others' distress or sadness.
I feel comfortable and confident in most social situations.
I often feel that others are judging me negatively in social settings.
I generally like the person I am when I'm interacting with others.
I am sometimes embarrassed by my own behavior in group settings.
I am comfortable speaking up in front of large groups of people.
I avoid situations where I might be the center of attention.
Meeting new people in social situations comes easily to me.
I feel awkward and self-conscious when I have to lead a group discussion.
I enjoy spending time in social gatherings and group activities.
I find prolonged social interaction draining.
I actively seek out opportunities to be around other people.
I prefer to spend my free time alone rather than with others.
I tend to have an enthusiastic and upbeat attitude most of the time.
People rarely describe me as particularly energetic or animated.
I am often the person who keeps a group's energy and spirits up.
My mood tends to be fairly flat and subdued on most days.
I am willing to forgive people who have treated me badly if they apologize sincerely.
I hold onto resentment toward people who have wronged me.
Once someone has apologized genuinely, I let go of what they did.
I find it difficult to move past feelings of anger toward someone who hurt me.
I try to be gentle when criticizing others, even when they need honest feedback.
I am comfortable giving people blunt, unfiltered feedback.
I choose my words carefully when discussing sensitive topics with others.
I can be quite harsh in my assessments of others when they have made mistakes.
I am open to changing my mind when someone presents a compelling argument.
I find it frustrating when people challenge my opinions and beliefs.
I try to see merit in different perspectives, even ones I disagree with.
I tend to stick firmly to my position once I have formed an opinion.
I stay calm and composed when dealing with slow or frustrating processes.
I get irritated quickly when people are inefficient or make repeated mistakes.
I handle delays and setbacks without losing my temper.
I find myself becoming annoyed when others don't do things correctly.
I keep my belongings and workspace tidy and well-organized.
Clutter and disorganization don't particularly bother me.
I prefer to have a clear system for managing my tasks and responsibilities.
My personal space is often messy or disorganized.
I work hard and consistently on tasks until they are completed.
I often lose momentum or interest in tasks before finishing them.
I put in significant effort even when no one is watching.
I sometimes take shortcuts rather than doing things thoroughly.
I have high standards for the quality of my own work.
I am generally satisfied with work that is good enough, even if it's not perfect.
I notice and am bothered by small mistakes or imperfections in my work.
I don't often go back to refine or polish work once I've completed it.
I think carefully about the consequences of my decisions before acting.
I sometimes act impulsively without thinking through the implications.
I consider long-term outcomes, not just immediate benefits, when making choices.
I tend to make decisions quickly, often without fully thinking them through.
I am moved or affected by beautiful music, visual art, or poetry.
I don't particularly notice or care about the aesthetic quality of my surroundings.
I find beauty in everyday things that others often overlook.
Art, music, and literature hold little interest for me.
I enjoy reading or learning about topics that have nothing to do with my daily life.
I am content with the knowledge I already have and rarely seek out new ideas.
I find myself wondering about how things work, even when the topic isn't practical.
I rarely seek out information beyond what's necessary for my immediate tasks.
I often come up with original ideas or novel approaches to problems.
I tend to stick to conventional methods rather than inventing new ones.
I find creative expression or experimentation genuinely satisfying.
People don't typically describe me as imaginative or creative.
I find myself drawn to ideas and people that don't fit conventional molds.
I prefer to follow established norms rather than challenge them.
I question accepted assumptions and conventional wisdom regularly.
I feel more comfortable with familiar, traditional ways of doing things.
